title: Abelia koehneana
taxonomy: Abelia koehneana is a species within the genus Abelia and the family Caprifoliaceae. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the order Dipsacales. This classification places Abelia koehneana within the larger group of plants known as angiosperms or flowering plants. Further classification at the kingdom level places Abelia koehneana within the Plantae kingdom. Abelia koehneana's classification is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, which includes both the genus name Abelia and the species name koehneana.
uses: Abelia koehneana has been used in traditional medicine in various regions. It is often employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential benefits and applications in modern medicine.

intro: Abelia koehneana is a flowering plant species, commonly known as the Chinese abelia, which is native to China and Taiwan. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Abelia koehneana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Abelia koehneana is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, to help alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as to promote overall well-being.
